summary: Re-test of CR-2026-05-30-002 (non-QR order block) on restaurant 698 after iter_2 P0 fixes. Backend Pydantic fix is confirmed at the DB layer (direct Mongo write reflects in GET /api/config/698 instantly). Guard C2 (MenuItems add-to-cart) and the rolling-cap diagnostics endpoint both work end-to-end. HOWEVER Guard C1 (Landing -> Browse Menu) still does NOT block at runtime even though the call site is now present in handleDiningMenuClick. Root cause is a NEW bug: LandingPage passes `selectedMode` into shouldBlockNonQrOrder, and selectedMode defaults to 'takeaway' on every landing render (useState init at L148), so the policy hits the HC6 takeaway/delivery bypass and returns block=false. Guard C2/C3 are unaffected because they do NOT pass selectedMode.

minor.issue: Could not be runtime-verified via curl from the test harness: POST /api/auth/login with {phone_or_email,password,user_type:'restaurant'} returned no access_token (login flow may need different shape from this host). Backend Pydantic fix IS in place per main agent note + grep, and DB-layer write reflects in GET, so the admin save path is presumed working — but a direct admin PUT round-trip was not exercised in this iteration.

ui_bugs.issue: shouldBlockNonQrOrder() IS now called at L490 (verified), but the call passes `selectedMode` (L497) AND selectedMode is initialised to 'takeaway' for every non-delivery render (L148: useState(scannedOrderType === 'delivery' ? 'delivery' : 'takeaway')). When the user lands on /698 with NO QR params, scannedOrderType is null, so selectedMode = 'takeaway'. The policy then hits `TAKEAWAY_OR_DELIVERY.has(ctx?.selectedMode)` in orderAccessPolicy.js L55 and returns { block:false, reason:'non-dinein-mode' }. Net result identical to the iter_2 regression: clicking Browse Menu on /698 (no QR, allowNonQrOrders=false) navigates to /698/password-setup instead of showing the Session Expired modal. Diagnostics POST count: 0. Reproduced live this iteration with allowNonQrOrders=false set via direct Mongo write.

integration_issues.issue: Because Guard C1 silently bypasses, the user proceeds to /password-setup. Cart is NOT cleared at C1 and the diagnostics 'landing' event is NEVER recorded — so Scenario 14 (telemetry shape for checkpoint='landing') cannot be exercised. Scenario 3 fails end-to-end.

action_items: MAIN AGENT P0 — Fix LandingPage.jsx handleDiningMenuClick: stop passing selectedMode into shouldBlockNonQrOrder when no QR / dine-in context. Two equivalent fixes: (a) preferred — pass `selectedMode: isTakeawayDeliveryMode ? selectedMode : null` (gates the field on the same flag that controls the OrderModeSelector UI). (b) alternative — drop selectedMode from the C1 ctx entirely; rely on scannedOrderType only (which mirrors what MenuItems C2 and ReviewOrder C3 already do — both ignore selectedMode and both work correctly). Either change unblocks Scenarios 3, 14, 15. Hot-reload safe. Diff is ~1 line.

MAIN AGENT — After the fix, re-verify Scenarios 3, 9 (takeaway mode bypass via OrderModeSelector — make sure (a) still allows takeaway to bypass), 10, 11 (716 carve-out via /716 with flag=false), 14 (payload shape on 'landing' POST), 15 (no POSTs when flag=true), 16 (skipOtp + allowNonQrOrders=true coexistence).

MAIN AGENT — Consider adding a unit test for orderAccessPolicy.js with the matrix {selectedMode default='takeaway', scannedOrderType=null, scannedTableId=null} -> expect block=true when flag is false. This would have caught the regression.
critical_code_review_comments: P0: LandingPage.jsx L497 incorrectly threads `selectedMode` into the policy. selectedMode is a UI-only state (drives OrderModeSelector for takeaway/delivery contexts) and defaults to 'takeaway' on every render — turning it into a stealth HC6 bypass. MenuItems.jsx L425-435 and ReviewOrder.jsx L824-833 deliberately do NOT include selectedMode for exactly this reason; C1 should match.

GOOD: server.py AppConfigUpdate Pydantic model is now updated (verified via direct DB write reflecting through GET /api/config/698 — the read path returns the field correctly). Backend persistence layer is wired.
GOOD: Guard C2 (MenuItems.jsx add-to-cart) works end-to-end: modal renders with correct testids, sendBeacon POST fires to /api/diagnostics/non-qr-block, body cleared, non-dismissable.
GOOD: Rolling cap enforcement: 250 concurrent POSTs against TEST_RID resulted in exactly 200 documents persisted in non_qr_blocks. The (rid, ts DESC) index + capped pruning logic works.
GOOD: NonQrBlockModal verified non-dismissable: backdrop click no-op, no Escape handler, body scroll locked, single CTA.
GOOD: orderAccessPolicy.js HC1 (716 carve-out), HC4 (default-allow), HC5 (edit-mode), HC7 (walkin scan) all correctly implemented and unit-readable.

MINOR: diagnosticsService.js uses navigator.sendBeacon first — this means Playwright's request.post_data returns None for the body. This is correct production behaviour (sendBeacon survives navigation) but it does make payload-shape assertions harder to runtime-verify. Consider exposing a `__forceFetch` debug knob or asserting payload via backend ingestion check (read the latest row for that restaurant_id after the click).

success_rate.backend: diagnostics endpoint + rolling cap + DB-layer config write: 100% (3/3 verified). Admin PUT round-trip via API not exercised this iteration.
success_rate.frontend: C2 add-to-cart: PASS. C3 place-order: code-correct (same shape as C2, not runtime-tested because C1 blocks runtime path). C1 landing: FAIL — selectedMode bypass bug. Modal/policy/admin section all individually correct. ~50% of feature actually works at runtime.

test_credentials: owner@cafeflora.com / Qplazm@10. Restaurant 698. /app/memory/test_credentials.md present and accurate.
seed_data_creation: Set customer_app_config.allowNonQrOrders=false on restaurant 698 via direct Mongo write for the test run. Posted 250 dummy diagnostics events with restaurant_id=TEST_RID to validate the 200-doc rolling cap (exactly 200 persisted — verified). Cleanup performed: $unset allowNonQrOrders on 698 AND 716 (716 was a no-op — field was already absent); delete_many({restaurant_id:'TEST_RID'}) on non_qr_blocks. Post-test GET /api/config/698 returns allowNonQrOrders=null (default).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Single P0 remaining: LandingPage.jsx handleDiningMenuClick passes `selectedMode` into shouldBlockNonQrOrder. Because useState at L148 defaults selectedMode to 'takeaway' on EVERY landing render (the operand is `scannedOrderType === 'delivery' ? 'delivery' : 'takeaway'`), the policy always hits the takeaway/delivery HC6 bypass and never blocks. Fix in one place: either gate the field as `selectedMode: isTakeawayDeliveryMode ? selectedMode : null` OR drop the field entirely from C1's ctx (matching what C2 and C3 already do). After the fix, re-run Scenarios 3, 9, 10, 11, 14, 15, 16. C2 (Scenario 4) and the 200-doc cap (Scenario 17) are already verified — do not need to retest. C3 (Scenario 5) is code-equivalent to C2 (no selectedMode passed) so it should pass after C1 fix unblocks the user-facing path. Note for runtime payload assertions (Scenario 14): sendBeacon Blobs are not visible via Playwright page.on('request') post_data. Either (a) read the last-inserted doc from db.non_qr_blocks for restaurant_id='698' after the click, or (b) temporarily monkey-patch navigator.sendBeacon to undefined in the test page before the click to force the fetch keepalive path. Final state of DB at end of this run: 698=default, 716=default, non_qr_blocks free of TEST_RID rows.
rca of the issue: Iter_2's missing call-site is now present but the call is fed a corrupted argument. selectedMode is a UI control state (drives the OrderModeSelector toggle for takeaway/delivery contexts only) and is initialised to 'takeaway' as a UI default even when the user is on a non-QR / non-takeaway / non-delivery landing. By threading that value into shouldBlockNonQrOrder, handleDiningMenuClick effectively tells the policy 'this is a takeaway order', which trips the HC6 bypass (TAKEAWAY_OR_DELIVERY check at orderAccessPolicy.js L55-58) and short-circuits the block. The two sibling guards (MenuItems C2, ReviewOrder C3) correctly omit selectedMode and are unaffected. Reproduction: set 698.allowNonQrOrders=false in Mongo, visit /698 (no QR), enter phone+name, click Browse Menu — observe navigation to /password-setup with zero POSTs to /api/diagnostics/non-qr-block. Mitigation: gate `selectedMode` on `isTakeawayDeliveryMode` (which itself is derived from scannedOrderType — the only legitimate source of 'this user is on takeaway/delivery') or remove it from the C1 payload entirely. Either change is single-line and hot-reload safe.
